items.title: Oracle PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools Missing Authentication for Critical Function Vulnerability
items.description: Oracle PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools contains a missing authentication for critical function vulnerability which could allow an unauthenticated attacker to obtain takeover of PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools.

priority.reason: Confirmed exploited (CISA KEV). Known ransomware campaign use. EPSS 92% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 9.8.

query_guidance.tanium_pattern: Use Installed Application Version["Oracle  PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools"] to find affected builds (or Installed Applications containing "Oracle  PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools" for a broader sweep). Question shape: Get Installed Application Version["Oracle  PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools"] from all machines.
query_guidance.rapid7_pattern: In InsightVM, filter the vulnerability view by CVE-2026-35273. If the check only returns a version/potential match, run a credentialed scan to confirm real exposure. Build a dynamic asset group on installed "Oracle  PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools" to track remediation.

impact.affected_versions: Oracle  PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools (specific versions not provided, assume all vulnerable until vendor clarifies)
impact.how_to_check: Inventory all Oracle  PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools installations and compare versions against the advisory. Review logs on matching hosts for unusual authentication or administrative actions.

items.title: Check Point Security Gateway Improper Authentication Vulnerability
items.description: Check Point Security Gateway contains an improper authentication vulnerability in IKEv1 key exchange that could allow an unauthenticated remote attacker to bypass user authentication and establish a remote access VPN connection without a valid user password.

priority.reason: Confirmed exploited (CISA KEV). Known ransomware campaign use. EPSS 70% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 9.3.

query_guidance.tanium_pattern: Check Point Security Gateway is typically a network appliance, not a Tanium-managed endpoint. If it isn't under Tanium, pivot to the Open Ports sensor on adjacent managed hosts to spot exposed management interfaces, and use your network/asset inventory to confirm firmware versions.
query_guidance.rapid7_pattern: In InsightVM, filter the vulnerability view by CVE-2026-50751. If the check only returns a version/potential match, run a credentialed scan to confirm real exposure. Build a dynamic asset group on installed "Check Point Security Gateway" to track remediation.

impact.affected_versions: Check Point Security Gateway (specific versions not provided, assume all vulnerable until vendor clarifies)
impact.how_to_check: Identify all internet-facing Check Point Security Gateway devices. Check firmware versions against the advisory, and review access logs for unauthorized access, unusual processes, or configuration changes.

items.title: Ivanti Sentry OS Command Injection Vulnerability
items.description: Ivanti Sentry (formerly known as MobileIron Sentry) contains an OS command injection vulnerability which could allow a remote unauthenticated user to achieve root-level remote code execution. This vulnerability can be successfully exploited in cases where the Sentry appliance is in an unmanaged state with its endpoints externally reachable. The use of mTLS with EPMM or restricted HTTPS access through Neurons for MDM makes interfaces inaccessible to external actors.

priority.reason: Confirmed exploited (CISA KEV). EPSS 99% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 10.0.

query_guidance.tanium_pattern: Use Installed Application Version["Ivanti Sentry"] to find affected builds (or Installed Applications containing "Ivanti Sentry" for a broader sweep). Question shape: Get Installed Application Version["Ivanti Sentry"] from all machines.
query_guidance.rapid7_pattern: In InsightVM, filter the vulnerability view by CVE-2026-10520. If the check only returns a version/potential match, run a credentialed scan to confirm real exposure. Build a dynamic asset group on installed "Ivanti Sentry" to track remediation.

impact.affected_versions: Ivanti Sentry (specific versions not provided, assume all vulnerable until vendor clarifies)
impact.how_to_check: Inventory all Ivanti Sentry installations and compare versions against the advisory. Review logs on matching hosts for unusual authentication or administrative actions.

items.title: Oracle WebLogic Server Unspecified Vulnerability
items.description: Oracle WebLogic contains an unspecified vulnerability that could allow an unauthenticated attacker with network access via T3, IIOP to compromise Oracle WebLogic Server. items.title: SimpleHelp Authentication Bypass Vulnerability
items.description: SimpleHelp contains an authentication bypass vulnerability in the OIDC authentication flow. When OIDC authentication is configured, identity tokens submitted during login are accepted without verifying their cryptographic signature. In a vulnerable configuration, a remote, unauthenticated attacker can submit a forged token containing arbitrary identity claims to obtain a fully authenticated technician session. In some configurations, this may also allow bypass of multi-factor authentication.

priority.reason: Confirmed exploited (CISA KEV). EPSS 1% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 10.0.

query_guidance.tanium_pattern: Use Installed Application Version["SimpleHelp  SimpleHelp"] to find affected builds (or Installed Applications containing "SimpleHelp  SimpleHelp" for a broader sweep). Question shape: Get Installed Application Version["SimpleHelp  SimpleHelp"] from all machines.

impact.how_to_check: Inventory all SimpleHelp  SimpleHelp installations and compare versions against the advisory. Review logs on matching hosts for unusual authentication or administrative actions.
